summary.intent: Replace the sparkles icon with a brain icon in the thinking/reasoning toggle, and add a distinctive visual indicator to reasoning-capable models in the model dropdown.
summary.outcome: Created a new brain SVG icon, wired it into the icon system, replaced the sparkles icon in the chat UI, and added a brain icon badge to reasoning-capable model items in the dropdown. Changes were committed and pushed.

repo: Icons use SVG mask files stored at crates/web/src/assets/icons/masks/ with hashed filenames (e.g. mask-459dd5c95de89b9a.svg), Heroicons style 24x24 viewbox, stroke-based.
CSS icon classes are defined in crates/web/ui/input.css and reference ./icons/masks/ relative to the Tailwind output path (crates/web/src/assets/css/style.css), not relative to input.css itself.

There are two style.css files: crates/web/src/assets/style.css (gitignored, generated) and crates/web/src/assets/css/style.css (tracked in git). The CLAUDE.md build command outputs to the gitignored path; the tracked one must be rebuilt separately.
Model dropdown items are built in crates/web/src/assets/js/models.js via a buildModelItem function. Reasoning capability is a known property on model objects.
The reasoning toggle uses icon-sparkles class in crates/web/src/assets/js/page-chat.js.

code.finding: buildModelItem constructs model dropdown entries; this is where the brain icon badge for reasoning-capable models was added.
Reasoning toggle button uses an icon class (changed from icon-sparkles to icon-brain).
Icon CSS classes are defined here; icon-brain class was added referencing mask-brain.svg.

workflow: When adding new icons, both the SVG mask file and the CSS class in input.css must be added, then Tailwind must be rebuilt to both output paths (gitignored and tracked).
Tailwind resolves CSS url() paths relative to the output file, not the input file — confirmed by comparing input.css location vs compiled style.css location.
Biome is used for JS linting and should be run on changed files before committing.

friction: Icon path resolution was confusing: input.css uses ./icons/masks/ but lives in ui/, while SVGs live in src/assets/icons/masks/ — required tracing the Tailwind build to understand URL resolution is relative to output, not input.
Two style.css files exist (one gitignored, one tracked) with no obvious indication from the CLAUDE.md build command which is which — caused an extra investigation and rebuild step.
No brain icon existed in the project, requiring creation from scratch rather than reusing an existing asset.
Icon filenames use opaque hashes rather than semantic names, making it hard to know what icons exist without listing the directory.
open_items: The brain SVG was created manually and may not perfectly match Heroicons style — visual QA of the icon rendering should be done.
Hash-based icon filenames vs the new mask-brain.svg (non-hashed) is an inconsistency that could cause confusion or break tooling that expects hashed names.
